label: Company (tenant UI)
description: A registered statutory body that owns accounting books, tax identity, and statutory obligations.

label: Organization unit
description: Operational node in the tree beneath a legal entity.

label: Workspace
description: Active operating snapshot: tenant, company, org, project selections.

label: Screen
description: Dot-separated module path for the active ERP screen.

label: Request data
description: Business information transmitted, separate from transport metadata.

label: Non-negotiable rule
description: Condition that must remain true under allowed state transitions.

label: Specification
description: Promised interface and behavior between producers and consumers.

label: Descriptive information (not enterprise knowledge)
description: Descriptive information about other data or assets.

label: Double-entry bookkeeping
description: Every transaction affects at least two accounts with equal debits and credits.

label: Accounting equation (A = L + E)
description: Assets equal liabilities plus equity at all times.

label: Company vs organization unit split
description: Afenda v2 decision to stop using organization as a catch-all label.

label: IFRS 10 (control
description: IFRS standard defining control for consolidation.

label: Tenant (SaaS customer boundary)
description: SaaS customer boundary — root security and subscription isolation unit.

label: Entity group
description: Corporate group structure under a tenant; may represent a holding group.

label: Ownership interest
description: Ownership relationship between legal entities for consolidation scope.

label: Consolidation scope
description: Scope of legal entities included in consolidated reporting.

label: Operating context
description: Server-resolved ERP operating snapshot composing tenant, legal entity, workspace, and scoped authorities.

label: Permission scope
description: Resolved permission evaluation scope for the current actor within tenant and workspace boundaries.

label: Project.
description: Optional project dimension scoping work, budgets, or delivery within a workspace.

label: Team.
description: Optional team dimension for collaborative ownership within a workspace.

label: Localization
description: Locale, currency, and formatting context applied to user-visible and statutory outputs.

label: Workflow context.
description: Optional workflow state dimension for approval and process-scoped operations.

label: Business reference number (customer no., employee no., …).
description: Tenant-governed human administrative number family — not a canonical kernel ID.

label: Human reference
description: Tenant-scoped human-readable reference governed separately from kernel canonical IDs.

label: Inventory item
description: Stock or material master record tracked for quantity, valuation, and warehouse placement.

label: Procurement requisition
description: Internal request to purchase goods or services before a purchase order is issued.

label: Module runtime identity
description: Governed declaration that a line-of-business capability exists with wire-catalog key, lifecycle phase, and owning authorities before filesystem or behavior.

label: Wire catalog key
description: Stable catalog identifier binding module foundation to platform wire vocabulary.

label: Module ownership contract
description: One-row-per-responsibility map declaring which platform domain owns wire vocabulary, meaning, runtime, persistence, ingress, permissions, and presentation.

label: Knowledge map status
description: Classification of whether a business term is accepted, proposed, wire-only, missing, ambiguous, or deferred before runtime behavior.

label: Operating context consumption
description: Requirement that protected module work uses assembled tenant, entity, and org scope from the ERP integration spine — never local reconstruction.

label: Permission binding
description: Registration proving every module permission key exists in wire vocabulary and every protected surface declares required permission.

label: Audit action map
description: Governed mapping from runtime mutations to platform audit vocabulary — no ad-hoc action strings.

label: Metadata surface binding
description: Proof that every UI route declares permission, operating-context requirement, and metadata slot authority.

label: Module readiness dimension
description: One attestable facet of module foundation such as authority, knowledge, ownership, integration spine, permissions, audit, metadata, UI, tests, or gates.

label: Foundation lifecycle phase
description: Module authorized for scaffold and contracts only — behavior blocked until readiness attestation.

label: Module ingress
description: Protected application entry layer that wires spine, authorization, server actions, and route policy — not business use cases.

label: Readiness report
description: Evidence table proving pass or fail per readiness dimension with gate linkage.

label: Module policy declaration
description: Declared module runtime policy for create, approve, post, and blocked operations — without UI logic or kernel mutations.

label: Module event catalog
description: Declared catalog of module business events prepared for outbox and integration — distinct from audit actions and dispatch mechanics.

label: Outbox requirement classification
description: Classification of whether each catalogued module event requires durable outbox delivery before integration runtime.

label: Purchase order
description: External commitment document issued to a supplier to procure goods or services — distinct from internal requisition.

label: Supplier
description: Trading partner master reference used in procurement — SupplierId wire identity without duplicating kernel parsers.

label: Procurement RFQ
description: Request for quotation sourcing document used to solicit supplier pricing before purchase order commitment.

label: Procurement sourcing method
description: Governed classification of how procurement selects suppliers and paths to commitment — competitive, directed, catalog, or auction.

label: Blanket agreement
description: Framework procurement agreement establishing terms under which release purchase orders may be issued.

label: Supplier quote
description: Supplier's priced response to a procurement sourcing invitation — evidence for award decision before purchase order.
